HomePage.memorialText: Memoriaali service is an online platform for receiving digital materials from citizens and businesses. The easy-to-use Memoriaali helps both individuals and companies save their materials in the service and streamlines the process of receiving and archiving electronic materials for memory institutions. /n With the service you can for example, upload images, text files, audio, or video directly online. Memoriaali makes the transfer of materials smooth and secure, and both the user and the organization can decide whether the material will be public or confidential. /n Memoriaali is designed so that it can be used by many different archives and memory institutions, even if they already have their own archiving systems. Organizations can also define what type of material they want to collect and interactively enrich their collections.
HomePage.savingText: Memoriaali allows saving various types of materials, such as images, text files, audio, and video. The process is supported by AI components that detect common scanning errors, including blank pages, post-it notes, and folded corners. /n Description of the material is assisted by AI, which can extract keywords from the text and identify different named entities. This makes the description faster and more accurate. /n To store materials, you need to register and log in to the service.
HomePage.browsingText: Memoriaali’s materials can be browsed easily through the search service, which brings together all items defined as public. The service is designed to ensure smooth browsing even when there is a large amount of material. /n Search can be refined using free-text queries and various filters that narrow results, for example, by material type. Searches can be targeted to all description fields in Memoriaali. /n In addition to browsing, materials can be commented on freely or suggestions can be made for changes and additions to their descriptive information. This way, descriptions can be enriched through crowdsourcing.

AddFiles.infoMessageHeader: Things to note about artificial intelligence components
AddFiles.infoMessage: The models underlying the components have been trained on specific training datasets, and therefore it is very possible that there may be shortcomings in the components’ performance when processing the material you add. Handwritten text is usually recognized incorrectly, and errors may also occur in the interpretation of typed text. Components that identify descriptive metadata from texts do not work with handwritten materials. In contrast, the component that detects blank pages or scanning errors also works with handwritten materials or photographs. For digitally born material, the limitations relate to the accuracy of the descriptive metadata and subject indexing components. Always check the content produced by artificial intelligence.
AddFiles.flawDetectionInfo: The models underlying the components have been trained on specific training datasets, and therefore it is very possible that there may be shortcomings in the components’ performance when processing the material you add. The component that detects blank pages or scanning errors also works with handwritten materials or photographs. Always check the content produced by artificial intelligence.
AddFiles.metadataDetectionInfo: The models underlying the components have been trained on specific training datasets, and therefore it is very possible that there may be shortcomings in the components’ performance when processing the material you add. Handwritten text is usually recognized incorrectly, and errors may also occur in the interpretation of typed text. Components that identify descriptive metadata from texts do not work with handwritten materials. For digitally born material, the limitations relate to the accuracy of the descriptive metadata and subject indexing components. Always check the content produced by artificial intelligence.

InfoAboutMemoriaali.loginRegisterText: To use the Memoriaali web service, you must first create a user account. You can register by clicking the Login button at the top of the page. On the login page, you will find the option to sign in or go to the registration page. To create an account, we need a unique username, an email address, and a password./n During registration, we will send an activation code to your email. You will be asked to enter this code during your first login. This confirms that your email address is valid and allows you to use Memoriaali. /n You can sign in using either your username or email address together with the password you created. If you forget your username or password, click the Forgot username or password? button and follow the instructions on the page.
InfoAboutMemoriaali.searchText: You can browse Memoriaali's materials through the search service, which gathers all public materials as well as materials shared with you. You can browse all materials in Memoriaali or refine your search using free-text search and various filters that narrow the results, for example by material type. The search can be targeted at all metadata fields in Memoriaali. /n To use the search service, your Memoriaali account must be activated. /n In the search view, you can also comment on materials and add metadata suggestions.
InfoAboutMemoriaali.saveMaterialText: You can add your own materials to Memoriaali through your personal workspace, either individually or in batches. Each material is supplemented with descriptive metadata, which helps other users find the content through search and browsing. You also define the material's visibility level. Even if the material is marked as private, Memoriaali administrators can still view it.
InfoAboutMemoriaali.aiComponentsText: Scanning error detection helps monitor the quality of the material. The AI components include detection of folded corners, blank pages, and post-it notes. You can use all of these or select only the components you want. It is also possible to disable the check entirely.
InfoAboutMemoriaali.oralHistoryText: In the oral history service, you can record audio or video in which you recount memories of events. You can use helper questions to support your recollection, and they will be visible to you during the recording.
